content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-5901          322-6441            322-6444

January 26, 1982


Allen Grimes, Esq.
City Attorney
City of Atascadero
P.O. Box 749
Atascadero, CA  93422

Dear Mr. Grimes:

     Thank you for your letter of December 31, 1981, requesting
advice from this office.  The following advice is provided
pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b).

     My understanding of the situation is as follows.  The
offices of the City of Atascadero are located in the Veterans
Memorial Building.  The County rents space from the City in
the building which it uses, in part, for the County Library.
One of the city councilmembers is employed by the County as
a part-time librarian.

     You asked whether there would be a conflict of interest
under the Political Reform Act, Sections 87100, et seq., for
the councilmember/librarian to participate in council decisions
involving the use of the City's space by the County Library.
These decisions include resolving whether or not the County
Library should be relocated so that the space can be used
for City uses.

     Based upon the facts given, I conclude that the councilmember
does not have a conflict of interest with respect to these
decisions.

     A conflict of interest exists when a public official
has a financial interest in a governmental decision to be
made by the decision-making body of which she is a member.
Section 87100.  An official has a financial interest within
the meaning of Section 87100 if the decision could foreseeably
have a material financial effect on:

___________________
1/All statutory references are to the Government Code.

Allen Grimes, Esq.
January 26, 1982
Page Two


     (a)  Any business entity in which the public official
has a direct or indirect investment worth more than one
thousand dollars($1,000).

     (b)  Any real property in which the public official
has a direct or indirect interest worth more than one
thousand dollars ($1,000).

     (c)  Any source of income, other than loans by a
commercial lending institution in the regular course of
business on terms available to the public without regard
to official status, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars
($250) or more in value provided to, received by or
promised to the public official within 12 months prior
to the time when the decision is made.

     (d)  Any business entity in which the public official
is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or
holds any position of management.

                                        Section 87103.

     The Political Reform Act specifically excludes from its
definition of income "[s]alary and reimbursements for expenses
or per diem received from a state, local or Federal government
agency...." Section 82030(b)(2). Inasmuch as the County
Library is a local government agency, the salary received by
the councilmember is not "income," and thus the County
is not a "source of income" to her. Accordingly, the councilmember
does not have to refrain from making or participating in
decisions involving the County Library when the only connection
between the councilmember and the County Library is the
employment relationship.

     I trust this letter will be helpful to you. Please
feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901, if you wish to
discuss this letter or if you have further questions.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Diane Maura Fishburn
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

DMF;plh

ROBERT J. WILKINS, JR.
MAYOR

WILLIAM H. STOVER
MAYOR PRO-TEMPORE

GEORGE P. HIGHLAND

MARJORIE B. MACKEY

ROLFE NELSON

MURRAY L. WARDEN
CITY MANAGER/CLERK

Atascadero
INCORPORATED JULY 2, 1979

CITY ATTORNEY
P. O. BOX 747
ATASCADERO, CA 93422
(805) 466-5678

POLICE DEPARTMENT
(805) 466-8600

ADMINISTRATION BUILDING
POST OFFICE BOX 747
ATASCADERO, CALIF. 93422

FIRE DEPARTMENT
6005 LEWIS AVENUE
ATASCADERO, CA 93422
(805) 466-2141

PHONE (805) 466-8000


December 31, 1981


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 Kay Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Attention: Conflict of Interest Section

Gentlemen:

I would like to request your opinion as to whether the following factual
situation constitutes a valid conflict of interest.

The City of Atascadero is a general law city, having five council members,
including a council-selected mayor. The City's offices are located in the
Veterans Memorial Building, which was constructed many years ago by the
developer of Atascadero. A number of charitable and County offices are
located in the Veterans Memorial Building, now sometimes called City Hall.
Thus, the County rents space from the City for a very reasonable rent. Part
of the space that is rented by the County is for the location of the adult
and children's library in the County library. One of the council members is em-
ployed by the County as a part-time librarian in the County library located
in the City Hall building.

On several occasions, the question has arisen as to whether the County library
should be relocated to a site other than the City Hall building so that the
space can be used for city space, such as for renovation and expansion of the
Police Department and/or other city uses.

The question arises as to whether there is any impediment as to this council
member voting on an issue that affects and involves her employer's agency,
to wit, the County library.

Thank you for your assistance.

Very truly yours,

ALLEN GRIMES
City Attorney

AG:fr

cc: Murray Warden, City Manager

sections.question: You asked whether there would be a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act, Sections 87100, et seq., for the council member/librarian to participate in council decisions involving the use of the City's space by the County Library. These decisions include resolving whether or not the County Library should be relocated so that the space can be used for City uses.
sections.conclusion: Based upon the facts given, I conclude that the council member does not have a conflict of interest with respect to these decisions. Since the County Library is a local government agency, the salary received by the councilmember is not 'income,' and thus the County is not a 'source of income' to her. Accordingly, the councilmember does not have to refrain from making or participating in decisions involving the County Library when the only connection between the councilmember and the County Library is the employment relationship.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a city council member employed part-time by the County as a librarian has a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act when voting on decisions regarding the County Library's use of space in City Hall, including potential relocation.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes no conflict of interest exists because salary from a local government agency is statutorily excluded from the definition of 'income' under the Political Reform Act, so the County Library employment does not create a disqualifying financial interest.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request letter (December 31, 1981) and FPPC response letter (January 26, 1982). Extracted information from FPPC response. Some text degradation in OCR but key sections are legible. Question and conclusion sections clearly identified and extracted verbatim from FPPC response.

QUESTION: You asked whether there would be a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act, Sections 87100, et seq., for the council member/librarian to participate in council decisions involving the use of the City's space by the County Library. These decisions include resolving whether or not the County Library should be relocated so that the space can be used for City uses.

CONCLUSION: Based upon the facts given, I conclude that the council member does not have a conflict of interest with respect to these decisions. Since the County Library is a local government agency, the salary received by the councilmember is not 'income,' and thus the County is not a 'source of income' to her. Accordingly, the councilmember does not have to refrain from making or participating in decisions involving the County Library when the only connection between the councilmember and the County Library is the employment relationship.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advised City Attorney Allen Grimes that a city council member employed part-time by the County as a librarian may participate in council votes regarding the County Library's space in City Hall without a conflict of interest, as government employment income is statutorily excluded from the Act's definition of income.
